title: Asianet (TV channel)

infobox.value: [1080i](/wiki/1080i "1080i") [HDTV](/wiki/HDTV "HDTV") (downscaled to [letterboxed](/wiki/Letterboxed "Letterboxed") [576i](/wiki/576i "576i") for the [SDTV](/wiki/SDTV "SDTV") feed)[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

is an Indian [Malayalam language](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") [general entertainment](/wiki/Generalist_channel "Generalist channel") [pay television](/wiki/Pay_television "Pay television") channel owned by [JioStar](/wiki/JioStar "JioStar"), a joint venture between [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") and [Disney India](/wiki/Disney_India "Disney India").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It primarily telecasts programs such as serials, [reality shows](/wiki/Reality_television "Reality television") and Malayalam films. Asianet HD is the first High Definition channel in Malayalam and was the Star network's first HD channel in a language other than Hindi or English.[[2]](#cite_note-asianetglobal.com-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indian Malayalam-language television channel

Television channel

Asianet

**Asianet** is an Indian [Malayalam language](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") [general entertainment](/wiki/Generalist_channel "Generalist channel") [pay television](/wiki/Pay_television "Pay television") channel owned by [JioStar](/wiki/JioStar "JioStar"), a joint venture between [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") and [Disney India](/wiki/Disney_India "Disney India").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It primarily telecasts programs such as serials, [reality shows](/wiki/Reality_television "Reality television") and Malayalam films. Asianet HD is the first High Definition channel in Malayalam and was the Star network's first HD channel in a language other than Hindi or English.[[2]](#cite_note-asianetglobal.com-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Founding

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Founding")]

The channel was founded by Raji Menon in 1993.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) In late 2006, Menon partially pulled out of Asianet Communications, turning over control to [Rajeev Chandrasekhar](/wiki/Rajeev_Chandrasekhar "Rajeev Chandrasekhar") (Jupiter Entertainment Ventures).[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) [Star India](/wiki/Disney_Star "Disney Star") bought a 51% stake in Asianet Communications and formed a joint venture with JEV in November 2008.[[6]](#cite_note-:114-6) In 2014, Star India took full ownership of Asianet Communications.[[7]](#cite_note-:7-7)

### Star India Acquisitions (2008–2014)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Star India Acquisitions (2008–2014)")]

Asianet was restructured into four companies in June 2008 (general entertainment, news, radio, and media infrastructure). This move was to allow separate investments in each company.[[7]](#cite_note-:7-7) Star India started talks with the owners of the Asianet channels in August 2008.[[6]](#cite_note-:114-6)

Star India eventually bought a 51% stake in Asianet Communications and formed a joint venture with JEV in November 2008.[[8]](#cite_note-:4-8) The joint venture, called "Star Jupiter", comprised all general entertainment channels of Asianet 8 October 2013 Bombay 12 March Communications (Asianet, [Asianet Plus](#Asianet_Plus), [Asianet Movies](#Asianet_Movies), [Asianet Suvarna](/wiki/Star_Suvarna "Star Suvarna") and Asianet Sitara, a Telugu language channel[[9]](#cite_note-9)) and [Vijay TV](/wiki/Star_Vijay "Star Vijay"). Star India had reportedly paid $235 million in cash for the 51% stake and assumed a net debt of approximately $20 million.[[7]](#cite_note-:7-7) It is not clear how much stake Raji Menon held in the new Star Jupiter venture. Before forming the JV, it was known that the founder (Raji Menon) had held about 26% stake.[[6]](#cite_note-:114-6) Star India increased its stake in Asianet Communications to 75% in July 2010 (for which Star India paid around $90 million in cash), and to 87%, by acquiring 12% stake for $160 million in June, 2013. The latter move was by acquiring a 19% equity stake in Vijay TV from Chandrasekhar and Asianet Communications MD Madhavan.[[7]](#cite_note-:7-7) Following the June 2013 investment, Asianet Communications was valued at $1.33 billion. Star India acquired a 100% stake in Asianet Communications (buying the remaining 13% stake) in March 2014.[[7]](#cite_note-:7-7)[[8]](#cite_note-:4-8)

**The Walt Disney Company 21st Century Fox Acquisitions (2019–2022)**

On 20 March 2019, following the acquisition of [21st Century Fox](/wiki/21st_Century_Fox "21st Century Fox") by [The Walt Disney Company](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company"), its Indian subsidiary Star India, Star India-owned **Asianet Communications** and Star India's remaining subsidiaries became a subsidiary of [Disney India](/wiki/Disney_India "Disney India").

### Merger with Viacom18 (2023–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Merger with Viacom18 (2023–present)")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Some of this section's [listed sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") **may not be [reliable](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources")**. Please help improve this article by looking for better, more reliable sources. Unreliable citations may be challenged and removed. *(November 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

In July 2023, The Walt Disney Company began exploring strategic options, including a sale or formation of a joint venture, for their businesses in India.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Between September and October 2023, the company held preliminary talks with [Reliance Industries](/wiki/Reliance_Industries "Reliance Industries"), billionaire [Gautam Adani](/wiki/Gautam_Adani "Gautam Adani") and [Kalanithi Maran](/wiki/Kalanithi_Maran "Kalanithi Maran"), owner of the [Sun Group](/wiki/Sun_Group "Sun Group"), for a potential sale of its streaming and linear television assets.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) In late October 2023, it was reported that Disney was nearing a cash and stock deal with Reliance Industries for the sale of its operations in India, including a controlling stake in Disney Star. The assets Reliance would acquire are reportedly valued at around $7–8 billion and a transaction was to be announced as early as November.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Although Disney CEO [Bob Iger](/wiki/Bob_Iger "Bob Iger") refuted claims of a sale during a third-quarter earnings call, it was announced in December 2023 that Disney and Reliance had signed a non-binding term sheet for a merger. According to its terms, Reliance would hold 51% of the merged company in cash and stock, while Disney would own the remaining 49%. For purposes of the merger, a subsidiary of [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") was created to absorb Disney Star through a [stock swap](/wiki/Stock_swap "Stock swap"),[[14]](#cite_note-Prasad-14) pending ratification and regulatory approval.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In February 2024, Disney and Reliance reached a deal to merge their streaming and television assets, with the joint venture valued at $8.5 billion including synergies. As part of the deal, Viacom18 would be merged into Disney Star with Disney holding a 36.84% stake in the combined entity, which would bring together assets such as linear television entertainment channels [StarPlus](/wiki/StarPlus "StarPlus"), [Colors TV](/wiki/Colors_TV "Colors TV") and the [Star Gold Network](/wiki/Star_Gold_Network "Star Gold Network"), sports channels [Star Sports](/wiki/Star_Sports_(India) "Star Sports (India)") and [Sports18](/wiki/Sports18 "Sports18") and the streaming services [JioCinema](/wiki/JioCinema "JioCinema") and [Disney+ Hotstar](/wiki/Disney%2B_Hotstar "Disney+ Hotstar") with [Nita Ambani](/wiki/Nita_Ambani "Nita Ambani") serving as the chairperson of the joint venture, with [Uday Shankar](/wiki/Uday_Shankar_(businessman) "Uday Shankar (businessman)") serving as vice chairperson with the deal giving 16.34% to Reliance and 46.82% to Viacom18, with the deal being expected to close sometime in late 2024 or early 2025 pending regulatory approval.[[14]](#cite_note-Prasad-14)[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

In March 2024, it was reported that [Paramount Global](/wiki/Paramount_Global "Paramount Global") was looking to sell its 13.01% stake in [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") to Reliance, which already owned a 73.91% share via [TV18](/wiki/TV18 "TV18").[[21]](#cite_note-21) Although [Bloomberg News](/wiki/Bloomberg_News "Bloomberg News") reported that the deal was unlikely to close, the deal was confirmed a week later for $517 million, its closure subject to regulatory approval and the completion of the joint venture between Viacom18 and Disney. Paramount would, however, continue to license its content to the company.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

In August 2024, the [National Company Law Tribunal](/wiki/National_Company_Law_Tribunal "National Company Law Tribunal") approved a deal between Disney and Reliance Industries where JioCinema and Viacom18 would be merged into Digital18.[[23]](#cite_note-23) In November 2024, Disney and Reliance Industries agreed to merge Star India and Viacom18. The deal was reported to be valued at $8.5 billion.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Sister channels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sister channels")]

### Asianet Plus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Asianet Plus")]

***Asianet Plus*** is the second [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") language [general entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment") [pay television](/wiki/Pay_television "Pay television") channel is owned by [JioStar](/wiki/JioStar "JioStar"), a Joint venture between [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") and [Disney India](/wiki/Disney_India "Disney India"). It broadcasts serials, re-broadcasts of Asianet's old serials, and various movies. [Indian Super League](/wiki/Indian_Super_League "Indian Super League") (ISL) is being broadcasting on Asianet Plus from 2018 and Kerala Cricket League (KCL) from 2025. It was earlier known for its Dubbed Hindi Serials *(eg:[Mounam Sammadham](/wiki/Iss_Pyaar_Ko_Kya_Naam_Doon%3F "Iss Pyaar Ko Kya Naam Doon?"))* and Youth oriented programs.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### Asianet Movies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Asianet Movies")]

***Asianet Movies*** is an Indian Malayalam language pay television [movie channel](/wiki/List_of_movie_television_channels "List of movie television channels") that was launched on 15 July 2012. It Replaced Asianet Sitara, a Telugu General Entertainment channel. owned by [JioStar](/wiki/JioStar "JioStar") a Joint venture between [Viacom18](/wiki/Viacom18 "Viacom18") and [Disney India](/wiki/Disney_India "Disney India").[[26]](#cite_note-26) The channel usually broadcasts movies that were previously broadcast on Asianet and also some premieres. Its HD feed, *Asianet Movies HD*, launched on 15 March 2023. Asianet Movies HD was the first HD movie channel in Malayalam.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

### Asianet Middle East

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Asianet Middle East")]

**Asianet Middle East** is a Malayalam-language television channel primarily targets the Malayali diaspora residing in the [Middle East](/wiki/Middle_East "Middle East"). The channel broadcasts a variety of entertainment content including serials, movies, and cultural programs, with scheduling tailored to [Gulf Standard Time](/wiki/Gulf_Standard_Time "Gulf Standard Time").[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Programming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Programming")]

Main article: [List of programs broadcast by Asianet](/wiki/List_of_programs_broadcast_by_Asianet "List of programs broadcast by Asianet")

## Awards ceremonies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asianet_(TV_channel)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Awards ceremonies")]

* [Asianet Film Awards](/wiki/Asianet_Film_Awards "Asianet Film Awards") is an award ceremony for films presented annually by Asianet. Asianet says that an awards ceremony has been instituted to honor both artistic and technical excellence in the [Malayalam language film industry](/wiki/Malayalam_cinema "Malayalam cinema").[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* [Asianet Television Awards](/wiki/Asianet_Television_Awards "Asianet Television Awards") for television serials were started with this name, every year best serials were nominated and presented awards to them.[[30]](#cite_note-30)
